children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: For purposes of , the term “accumulated funding deficiency” for a CSEC plan means the excess of the total charges to the funding standard account for all plan years (beginning with the first plan year to which applies) over the total credits to such account for such years or, if less, the excess of the total charges to the alternative minimum funding standard account for such plan years over the total credits to such account for such years.section 1082 of this titlesection 1082 of this title

children.heading: Account required
children.label: 1 Account required
children.text: Each plan to which this section applies shall establish and maintain a funding standard account. Such account shall be credited and charged solely as provided in this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the normal cost of the plan for the plan year,

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a plan in existence on , the unfunded past service liability under the plan on the first day of the first plan year to which applies, over a period of 40 plan years,January 1, 1974section 1082 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a plan which comes into existence after , but before the first day of the first plan year beginning after , the unfunded past service liability under the plan on the first day of the first plan year to which applies, over a period of 30 plan years,January 1, 1974December 31, 2013section 1082 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: separately, with respect to each plan year, the net increase (if any) in unfunded past service liability under the plan arising from plan amendments adopted in such year, over a period of 15 plan years,

children.label: iv
children.text: separately, with respect to each plan year, the net experience loss (if any) under the plan, over a period of 5 plan years, and

children.label: v
children.text: separately, with respect to each plan year, the net loss (if any) resulting from changes in actuarial assumptions used under the plan, over a period of 10 plan years,

children.label: C
children.text: the amount necessary to amortize each waived funding deficiency (within the meaning of ) for each prior plan year in equal annual installments (until fully amortized) over a period of 5 plan years,section 1082(c)(3) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the amount necessary to amortize in equal annual installments (until fully amortized) over a period of 5 plan years any amount credited to the funding standard account under paragraph (3)(D), and

children.label: E
children.text: the amount necessary to amortize in equal annual installments (until fully amortized) over a period of 20 years the contributions which would be required to be made under the plan but for the provisions of (as in effect on the day before ).section 1082(c)(7)(A)(i)(I) of this titleAugust 17, 2006

children.label: A
children.text: the amount considered contributed by the employer to or under the plan for the plan year,

children.label: i
children.text: separately, with respect to each plan year, the net decrease (if any) in unfunded past service liability under the plan arising from plan amendments adopted in such year, over a period of 15 plan years,

children.label: ii
children.text: separately, with respect to each plan year, the net experience gain (if any) under the plan, over a period of 5 plan years, and

children.label: iii
children.text: separately, with respect to each plan year, the net gain (if any) resulting from changes in actuarial assumptions used under the plan, over a period of 10 plan years,

children.label: C
children.text: the amount of the waived funding deficiency (within the meaning of ) for the plan year, andsection 1082(c)(3) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of a plan year for which the accumulated funding deficiency is determined under the funding standard account if such plan year follows a plan year for which such deficiency was determined under the alternative minimum funding standard, the excess (if any) of any debit balance in the funding standard account (determined without regard to this subparagraph) over any debit balance in the alternative minimum funding standard account.

children.heading: Combining and offsetting amounts to be amortized
children.label: 4 Combining and offsetting amounts to be amortized
A
children.text: may be combined into one amount under such paragraph to be amortized over a period determined on the basis of the remaining amortization period for all items entering into such combined amount, and

children.label: B
children.text: may be offset against amounts required to be amortized under the other such paragraph, with the resulting amount to be amortized over a period determined on the basis of the remaining amortization periods for all items entering into whichever of the two amounts being offset is the greater.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the funding standard account (and items therein) shall be charged or credited (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury) with interest at the appropriate rate consistent with the rate or rates of interest used under the plan to determine costs.

children.label: i
children.text: 150 percent of the Federal mid-term rate (as in effect under for the 1st month of such plan year), orsection 1274 of title 26

children.label: ii
children.text: the rate of interest determined under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Amortization schedules in effect
children.label: 6 Amortization schedules in effect
children.text: Amortization schedules for amounts described in paragraphs (2) and (3) that are in effect as of the last day of the last plan year beginning before , by reason of section 104 of the Pension Protection Act of 2006 shall remain in effect pursuant to their terms and this section, except that such amounts shall not be amortized again under this section.January 1, 2014

children.heading: Determinations to be made under funding method
children.label: 1 Determinations to be made under funding method
children.text: For purposes of this section, normal costs, accrued liability, past service liabilities, and experience gains and losses shall be determined under the funding method used to determine costs under the plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the value of the plan’s assets shall be determined on the basis of any reasonable actuarial method of valuation which takes into account fair market value and which is permitted under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Dedicated bond portfolio
children.label: B Dedicated bond portfolio
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may by regulations provide that the value of any dedicated bond portfolio of a plan shall be determined by using the interest rate under (as in effect on the day before ).section 1082(b)(5) of this titleAugust 17, 2006

children.label: A
children.text: each of which is reasonable (taking into account the experience of the plan and reasonable expectations), and

children.label: B
children.text: which, in combination, offer the actuary’s best estimate of anticipated experience under the plan.

children.heading: Treatment of certain changes as experience gain or loss
children.label: 4 Treatment of certain changes as experience gain or loss
A
children.text: a change in benefits under the Social Security Act [ et seq.] or in other retirement benefits created under Federal or State law, or42 U.S.C. 301

children.label: B
children.text: a change in the definition of the term “wages” under or a change in the amount of such wages taken into account under regulations prescribed for purposes of section 401(a)(5) of such title,section 3121 of title 26

children.heading: Funding methods available
children.label: A Funding methods available
children.text: All funding methods available to CSEC plans under (as in effect on the day before ) shall continue to be available under this section.section 1082 of this titleAugust 17, 2006

children.heading: Changes
children.label: B Changes
children.text: If the funding method for a plan is changed, the new funding method shall become the funding method used to determine costs and liabilities under the plan only if the change is approved by the Secretary of the Treasury. If the plan year for a plan is changed, the new plan year shall become the plan year for the plan only if the change is approved by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Approval required for certain changes in assumptions by certain single-employer plans subject to additional funding requirement
children.label: C Approval required for certain changes in assumptions by certain single-employer plans subject to additional funding requirement

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No actuarial assumption (other than the assumptions described in subsection (h)(3)) used to determine the current liability for a plan to which this subparagraph applies may be changed without the approval of the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Plans to which subparagraph applies
children.label: ii Plans to which subparagraph applies
I
children.text: the plan is a CSEC plan,

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate unfunded vested benefits as of the close of the preceding plan year (as determined under ) of such plan and all other plans maintained by the contributing sponsors (as defined in ) and members of such sponsors’ controlled groups (as defined in ) which are covered by subchapter III (disregarding plans with no unfunded vested benefits) exceed $50,000,000, andsection 1306(a)(3)(E)(iii) of this titlesection 1301(a)(13) of this titlesection 1301(a)(14) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: the change in assumptions (determined after taking into account any changes in interest rate and mortality table) results in a decrease in the funding shortfall of the plan for the current plan year that exceeds $50,000,000, or that exceeds $5,000,000 and that is 5 percent or more of the current liability of the plan before such change.

children.label: A
children.text: the funding standard account shall be credited with the amount of such excess, and

children.label: B
children.text: all amounts described in paragraphs (2)(B), (C), and (D) and (3)(B) of subsection (b) which are required to be amortized shall be considered fully amortized for purposes of such paragraphs.

children.label: A
children.text: the accrued liability (including normal cost) under the plan (determined under the entry age normal funding method if such accrued liability cannot be directly calculated under the funding method used for the plan), over

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of the plan’s assets, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the value of such assets determined under paragraph (2).

children.label: I
children.text: 90 percent of the current liability (determined without regard to paragraph (4) of subsection (h)) of the plan (including the expected increase in such current liability due to benefits accruing during the plan year), over

children.label: II
children.text: the value of the plan’s assets determined under paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Assets
children.label: ii .—Assets
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), assets shall not be reduced by any credit balance in the funding standard account.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, a determination of experience gains and losses and a valuation of the plan’s liability shall be made not less frequently than once every year, except that such determination shall be made more frequently to the extent required in particular cases under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Current year
children.label: i Current year
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the valuation referred to in subparagraph (A) shall be made as of a date within the plan year to which the valuation refers or within one month prior to the beginning of such year.

children.heading: Use of prior year valuation
children.label: ii Use of prior year valuation
children.text: The valuation referred to in subparagraph (A) may be made as of a date within the plan year prior to the year to which the valuation refers if, as of such date, the value of the assets of the plan are not less than 100 percent of the plan’s current liability.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: iii Adjustments
children.text: Information under clause (ii) shall, in accordance with regulations, be actuarially adjusted to reflect significant differences in participants.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iv Limitation
children.text: A change in funding method to use a prior year valuation, as provided in clause (ii), may not be made unless as of the valuation date within the prior plan year, the value of the assets of the plan are not less than 125 percent of the plan’s current liability.

children.heading: Time when certain contributions deemed made
children.label: 9 Time when certain contributions deemed made
A
children.text: beginning on the day after the last day of such plan year, and

children.label: B
children.text: ending on the day which is 8½ months after the close of the plan year,

children.heading: Anticipation of benefit increases effective in the future
children.label: 10 Anticipation of benefit increases effective in the future
children.text: In determining projected benefits, the funding method of a collectively bargained CSEC plan described in shall anticipate benefit increases scheduled to take effect during the term of the collective bargaining agreement applicable to the plan.section 413(a) of title 26
a substantial risk to the voluntary continuation of the plan, or
a substantial curtailment of pension benefit levels or employee compensation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A CSEC plan which uses a funding method that requires contributions in all years not less than those required under the entry age normal funding method may maintain an alternative minimum funding standard account for any plan year. Such account shall be credited and charged solely as provided in this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: the lesser of normal cost under the funding method used under the plan or normal cost determined under the unit credit method,

children.label: ii
children.text: the excess, if any, of the present value of accrued benefits under the plan over the fair market value of the assets, and

children.label: iii
children.text: an amount equal to the excess (if any) of credits to the alternative minimum standard account for all prior plan years over charges to such account for all such years, and

children.label: B
children.text: credited with the amount considered contributed by the employer to or under the plan for the plan year.

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 3 Interest
children.text: The alternative minimum funding standard account (and items therein) shall be charged or credited with interest in the manner provided under subsection (b)(5) with respect to the funding standard account.

children.label: A
children.text: 175 percent of the Federal mid-term rate (as in effect under for the 1st month of such plan year), orsection 1274 of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: the rate of interest used under the plan in determining costs.

children.heading: Amount of underpayment, period of underpayment
children.label: 2 Amount of underpayment, period of underpayment
i
children.text: the required installment, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount (if any) of the installment contributed to or under the plan on or before the due date for the installment.

children.heading: Period of underpayment
children.label: B Period of underpayment
children.text: The period for which interest is charged under this subsection with regard to any portion of the underpayment shall run from the due date for the installment to the date on which such portion is contributed to or under the plan (determined without regard to subsection (c)(9)).

children.heading: Order of crediting contributions
children.label: C Order of crediting contributions
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii), contributions shall be credited against unpaid required installments in the order in which such installments are required to be paid.

children.heading: Number of required installments; due dates
children.label: 3 Number of required installments; due dates

children.heading: Payable in 4 installments
children.label: A Payable in 4 installments
children.text: There shall be 4 required installments for each plan year.

children.heading: Time for payment of installments
children.label: B Time for payment of installments
children.text: In the case of the following required installments:The due date is:1stApril 152ndJuly 153rdOctober 154thJanuary 15 of the following year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The amount of any required installment shall be 25 percent of the required annual payment.

children.label: i
children.text: 90 percent of the amount required to be contributed to or under the plan by the employer for the plan year under (without regard to any waiver under subsection (c) thereof), orsection 1082 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: 100 percent of the amount so required for the preceding plan year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A plan to which this paragraph applies shall be treated as failing to pay the full amount of any required installment to the extent that the value of the liquid assets paid in such installment is less than the liquidity shortfall (whether or not such liquidity shortfall exceeds the amount of such installment required to be paid but for this paragraph).

children.heading: Plans to which paragraph applies
children.label: B Plans to which paragraph applies
i
children.text: is required to pay installments under this subsection for a plan year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: has a liquidity shortfall for any quarter during such plan year.

children.heading: Period of underpayment
children.label: C Period of underpayment
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), any portion of an installment that is treated as not paid under subparagraph (A) shall continue to be treated as unpaid until the close of the quarter in which the due date for such installment occurs.

children.heading: Limitation on increase
children.label: D Limitation on increase
children.text: If the amount of any required installment is increased by reason of subparagraph (A), in no event shall such increase exceed the amount which, when added to prior installments for the plan year, is necessary to increase the funded current liability percentage (taking into account the expected increase in current liability due to benefits accruing during the plan year) to 100 percent.

children.heading: Liquidity shortfall
children.label: i Liquidity shortfall
children.text: The term “liquidity shortfall” means, with respect to any required installment, an amount equal to the excess (as of the last day of the quarter for which such installment is made) of the base amount with respect to such quarter over the value (as of such last day) of the plan’s liquid assets.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “base amount” means, with respect to any quarter, an amount equal to 3 times the sum of the adjusted disbursements from the plan for the 12 months ending on the last day of such quarter.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: II Special rule
children.text: If the amount determined under subclause (I) exceeds an amount equal to 2 times the sum of the adjusted disbursements from the plan for the 36 months ending on the last day of the quarter and an enrolled actuary certifies to the satisfaction of the Secretary of the Treasury that such excess is the result of nonrecurring circumstances, the base amount with respect to such quarter shall be determined without regard to amounts related to those nonrecurring circumstances.

children.heading: Disbursements from the plan
children.label: iii Disbursements from the plan
children.text: The term “disbursements from the plan” means all disbursements from the trust, including purchases of annuities, payments of single sums and other benefits, and administrative expenses.

children.label: I
children.text: the plan’s funded current liability percentage for the plan year, and

children.label: II
children.text: the sum of the purchases of annuities, payments of single sums, and such other disbursements as the Secretary of the Treasury shall provide in regulations.

children.heading: Liquid assets
children.label: v Liquid assets
children.text: The term “liquid assets” means cash, marketable securities and such other assets as specified by the Secretary of the Treasury in regulations.

children.heading: Quarter
children.label: vi Quarter
children.text: The term “quarter” means, with respect to any required installment, the 3-month period preceding the month in which the due date for such installment occurs.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: F Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe such regulations as are necessary to carry out this paragraph.

children.heading: Fiscal years
children.label: A Fiscal years
children.text: In applying this subsection to a plan year beginning on any date other than January 1, there shall be substituted for the months specified in this subsection, the months which correspond thereto.

children.heading: Short plan year
children.label: B Short plan year
children.text: This subsection shall be applied to plan years of less than 12 months in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Imposition of lien where failure to make required contributions
children.label: g Imposition of lien where failure to make required contributions
A
children.text: any person fails to make a required installment under subsection (f) or any other payment required under this section before the due date for such installment or other payment, and

children.label: B
children.text: the unpaid balance of such installment or other payment (including interest), when added to the aggregate unpaid balance of all preceding such installments or other payments for which payment was not made before the due date (including interest), exceeds $1,000,000,

children.heading: Plans to which subsection applies
children.label: 2 Plans to which subsection applies
children.text: This subsection shall apply to a CSEC plan for any plan year for which the funded current liability percentage of such plan is less than 100 percent. This subsection shall not apply to any plan to which does not apply (as such section is in effect on ).section 1321 of this titleDecember 8, 1994

children.label: A
children.text: for plan years beginning after 1987, and

children.label: B
children.text: for which payment has not been made before the due date.

children.heading: Notice of failure
children.label: A Notice of failure
children.text: A person committing a failure described in paragraph (1) shall notify the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation of such failure within 10 days of the due date for the required installment or other payment.

children.heading: Period of lien
children.label: B Period of lien
children.text: The lien imposed by paragraph (1) shall arise on the due date for the required installment or other payment and shall continue until the last day of the first plan year in which the plan ceases to be described in paragraph (1)(B). Such lien shall continue to run without regard to whether such plan continues to be described in paragraph (2) during the period referred to in the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Certain rules to apply
children.label: C Certain rules to apply
children.text: Any amount with respect to which a lien is imposed under paragraph (1) shall be treated as taxes due and owing the United States and rules similar to the rules of subsections (c), (d), and (e) of shall apply with respect to a lien imposed by subsection (a) and the amount with respect to such lien.section 1368 of this title1So in original. Probably should be “paragraph (1)”.1

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 5 Enforcement
children.text: Any lien created under paragraph (1) may be perfected and enforced only by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, or at the direction of the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, by any contributing employer (or any member of the controlled group of the contributing employer).

children.heading: Due date; required installment
children.label: A Due date; required installment
children.text: The terms “due date” and “required installment” have the meanings given such terms by subsection (f), except that in the case of a payment other than a required installment, the due date shall be the date such payment is required to be made under this section.

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: B Controlled group
children.text: The term “controlled group” means any group treated as a single employer under subsections (b), (c), (m), and () of .osection 414 of title 26

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “current liability” means all liabilities to employees and their beneficiaries under the plan.

children.heading: Treatment of unpredictable contingent event benefits
children.label: 2 Treatment of unpredictable contingent event benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), any unpredictable contingent event benefit shall not be taken into account until the event on which the benefit is contingent occurs.

children.label: i
children.text: age, service, compensation, death, or disability, or

children.label: ii
children.text: an event which is reasonably and reliably predictable (as determined by the Secretary of the Treasury).

children.heading: Interest rate and mortality assumptions used
children.label: 3 Interest rate and mortality assumptions used

children.heading: Interest rate
children.label: A Interest rate
children.text: The rate of interest used to determine current liability under this section shall be the third segment rate determined under .section 1083(h)(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: Secretarial authority
children.label: i Secretarial authority
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may by regulation prescribe mortality tables to be used in determining current liability under this subsection. Such tables shall be based upon the actual experience of pension plans and projected trends in such experience. In prescribing such tables, the Secretary of the Treasury shall take into account results of available independent studies of mortality of individuals covered by pension plans.

children.heading: Periodic review
children.label: ii Periodic review
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall periodically (at least every 5 years) review any tables in effect under this subsection and shall, to the extent the Secretary of the Treasury determines necessary, by regulation update the tables to reflect the actual experience of pension plans and projected trends in such experience.

children.heading: Separate mortality tables for the disabled
children.label: C Separate mortality tables for the disabled

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of plan years beginning after , the Secretary of the Treasury shall establish mortality tables which may be used (in lieu of the tables under subparagraph (B)) to determine current liability under this subsection for individuals who are entitled to benefits under the plan on account of disability. The Secretary of the Treasury shall establish separate tables for individuals whose disabilities occur in plan years beginning before , and for individuals whose disabilities occur in plan years beginning on or after such date.December 31, 1995January 1, 1995

children.heading: Special rule for disabilities occurring after 1994
children.label: ii Special rule for disabilities occurring after 1994
children.text: In the case of disabilities occurring in plan years beginning after , the tables under clause (i) shall apply only with respect to individuals described in such subclause who are disabled within the meaning of title II of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] and the regulations thereunder.December 31, 199442 U.S.C. 401

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a participant to whom this paragraph applies, only the applicable percentage of the years of service before such individual became a participant shall be taken into account in computing the current liability of the plan.

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: B Applicable percentage
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the applicable percentage shall be determined as follows:If the years of participation are:The applicable percentage is:1202403604805 or more100.

children.heading: Participants to whom paragraph applies
children.label: C Participants to whom paragraph applies
i
children.text: has not accrued any other benefit under any defined benefit plan (whether or not terminated) maintained by the employer or a member of the same controlled group of which the employer is a member,

children.label: ii
children.text: who first becomes a participant under the plan in a plan year beginning after , andDecember 31, 1987

children.label: iii
children.text: has years of service greater than the minimum years of service necessary for eligibility to participate in the plan.

children.heading: Election
children.label: D Election
children.text: An employer may elect not to have this subparagraph apply. Such an election, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury.
the value of the plan’s assets determined under subsection (c)(2), is of
the current liability under the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount described in subsection (a), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the excess of the normal cost of the plan for the plan year over the amount actually contributed to or under the plan for the plan year.

children.heading: Normal cost
children.label: B Normal cost
children.text: In the case of a CSEC plan that uses a spread gain funding method, for purposes of this subsection, the term “normal cost” means normal cost as determined under the entry age normal funding method.

children.heading: Plan amendments
children.label: 2 Plan amendments
children.text: In the case of a CSEC plan that is in funding restoration status for a plan year, no amendment to such plan may take effect during such plan year if such amendment has the effect of increasing liabilities of the plan by means of increases in benefits, establishment of new benefits, changing the rate of benefit accrual, or changing the rate at which benefits become nonforfeitable. This paragraph shall not apply to any plan amendment that is required to comply with any applicable law. This paragraph shall cease to apply with respect to any plan year, effective as of the first day of the plan year (or if later, the effective date of the amendment) upon payment by the plan sponsor of a contribution to the plan (in addition to any contribution required under this section without regard to this paragraph) in an amount equal to the increase in the funding liability of the plan attributable to the plan amendment.

children.heading: Funding restoration plan
children.label: 3 Funding restoration plan
children.text: The sponsor of a CSEC plan shall establish a written funding restoration plan within 180 days of the receipt by the plan sponsor of a certification from the plan actuary that the plan is in funding restoration status for a plan year. Such funding restoration plan shall consist of actions that are calculated, based on reasonably anticipated experience and reasonable actuarial assumptions, to increase the plan’s funded percentage to 100 percent over a period that is not longer than the greater of 7 years or the shortest amount of time practicable. Such funding restoration plan shall take into account contributions required under this section (without regard to this paragraph). If a plan remains in funding restoration status for 2 or more years, such funding restoration plan shall be updated each year after the 1st such year within 180 days of receipt by the plan sponsor of a certification from the plan actuary that the plan remains in funding restoration status for the plan year.

children.label: A
children.text: the plan’s funding liability, based on the funding liability of the plan for the preceding plan year and on reasonable actuarial estimates, assumptions, and methods, and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of any contributions reasonably anticipated to be made for the preceding plan year.

children.heading: Funding restoration status
children.label: A Funding restoration status
children.text: A CSEC plan shall be treated as in funding restoration status for a plan year if the plan’s funded percentage as of the beginning of such plan year is less than 80 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: the value of plan assets (as determined under subsection (c)(2)), bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the plan’s funding liability.

children.heading: Funding liability
children.label: C Funding liability
children.text: The term “funding liability” for a plan year means the present value of all benefits accrued or earned under the plan as of the beginning of the plan year, based on the assumptions used by the plan pursuant to this section, including the interest rate described in subsection (b)(5)(A) (without regard to subsection (b)(5)(B)).

children.heading: Spread gain funding method
children.label: D Spread gain funding method
children.text: The term “spread gain funding method” has the meaning given such term under rules and forms issued by the Secretary of the Treasury.
